seoTitle: Hampton Park vs Happy Valley — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hampton Park and Happy Valley. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hampton Park vs Happy Valley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hampton Park and Happy Valley.
heading: Hampton Park vs Happy Valley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hampton Park, Victoria and Happy Valley, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hampton Park has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Happy Valley (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hampton Park or Happy Valley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hampton Park has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Happy Valley scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hampton Park scores 31/100 for bushfire risk. Happy Valley scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hampton Park or Happy Valley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hampton Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
